Name: curvature-aware_precision_preconditioning
Title: Curvature-Aware Precision Preconditioning for Efficient Gradient Optimization
Short Hypothesis: By dynamically adjusting preconditioner precision based on local curvature diagnostics, we can achieve faster convergence and better numerical stability in gradient optimization without additional training overhead.
Related Work: Existing work on adaptive preconditioning (e.g., AdaHessian, Shampoo) often relies on fixed heuristics or expensive curvature estimates. Methods like Spectral Latent Steering (SLS) explore subspace projections but do not address preconditioner precision. Our proposal bridges this gap by integrating curvature-aware precision routing with deterministic preconditioning, offering a mathematically grounded and computationally efficient alternative.
Abstract: Gradient preconditioning is a powerful technique for accelerating optimization in deep learning, but existing methods often suffer from suboptimal convergence and numerical instability due to fixed precision or heuristic curvature estimates. We propose Curvature-Aware Precision Preconditioning (CAPP), a novel framework that dynamically adjusts preconditioner precision based on local curvature diagnostics. By leveraging spectral subspace projections and deterministic curvature proxies, CAPP ensures computational efficiency and numerical stability while maintaining rigorous theoretical guarantees. Our approach replaces heuristic precision adjustments with curvature-informed precision routing, enabling faster convergence and better generalization. We validate CAPP on benchmark optimization tasks, demonstrating significant improvements in both convergence speed and solution quality compared to state-of-the-art preconditioning methods.
Experiments: Experiment 1: Compare CAPP with AdaHessian and Shampoo on benchmark optimization tasks (e.g., Rosenbrock, CIFAR-10). Metrics: convergence speed, solution quality, numerical stability.
Experiment 2: Evaluate the impact of curvature-aware precision routing on optimization trajectories. Metrics: curvature alignment, precision switching frequency, gradient covariance decay.
Experiment 3: Test CAPP on large-scale deep learning tasks (e.g., ImageNet). Metrics: training time, validation accuracy, computational overhead.
Experiment 4: Analyze the numerical stability of CAPP under varying precision levels. Metrics: condition number, Hessian perturbation sensitivity.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Complexity of curvature diagnostics may introduce computational overhead.
Precision routing may not generalize well to highly non-convex landscapes.
Theoretical guarantees may not hold in stochastic optimization settings.
Implementation challenges in integrating with existing deep learning frameworks.
